text.title: MCP Server Development Guide Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the mcp methodology end-to-end.

step.name: scope-the-server
step.action: Decide tool set (≤ 20). If > 20, split into focused servers.
step.decision-gate: If > 20 tools, STOP and split first.

step.name: pick-transport-and-scaffold
step.input: Tool list + deployment model (local vs remote)
step.action: Local installation → stdio. Remote / multi-tenant → streamable-http. Scaffold via SDK init (`npm init mcp-server` or Python equivalent).

step.name: implement-tools-with-schema
step.action: For each tool: Zod/Pydantic schema, structured description (see tool-description-as-prompt methodology), single-responsibility implementation.

step.name: wire-credentials-min-scope
step.action: Pass secrets via env vars; document `claude mcp add ... -e SECRET=...` line. Apply minimum scope (read-only if possible).

step.name: smoke-test-and-publish
step.action: Run mcp-inspector against every tool. Confirm schema + sample call. Publish to npm / PyPI when all green.
step.output: Published package + install command in README.
step.decision-gate: If smoke test fails on any tool, do NOT publish — fix the tool first.
